title: coelocline parviflora
intro: Coelocline parviflora, also known as the small-flowered coelocline, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the tropical regions of Australia and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Coelocline parviflora include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Coelocline parviflora include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent.
